revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 月間神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[南伊豆町]]手石336 |位置 = {{coord|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 月間神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[南伊豆町]]手石336 |位置 = {{coord|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]（[[事代主命]]） |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 伝・[[嘉祥]]年間（848年-851年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月2日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=月間神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''月間神社'''（つきまじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[南伊豆町]]手石（ていし）にある[[神社]]<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 南伊豆町の南東、青野川西岸である手石地区の中央に鎮座している。
創建は[[嘉祥]]年間（848年-851年）とされ、[[式内社]]である'''竹麻神社'''（たかま(つくまの)じんじゃ）三座中の一座（祭神は[[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]（[[事代主命]]））に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 月まの明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
伝承では、[[承和]]5年（838年）頃に[[神津島]]で大噴火が起きたことを受け、この地に遥拝所を建立することになり、ここには[[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]（[[事代主命]]）を祀り、その正后・阿波咩命は[[八幡神社 (下田市吉佐美)|吉佐美（現・八幡神社 合祀 三島神社）]]に、その子・物忌奈命は[[若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)|湊（現・若宮神社）]]にそれぞれ祀られたとされる<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
[[明治]]16年（[[1883年]]）に郷社に列する<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]（[[事代主命]]）
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[八幡神社 (下田市吉佐美)]] * [[若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{DEFAULTSORT:つきましんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:南伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:南伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

== 概要 == 南伊豆町の南東、青野川西岸である手石地区の中央に鎮座している。
創建は[[嘉祥]]年間（848年-851年）とされ、[[式内社]]である'''竹麻神社'''（たかま(つくまの)じんじゃ）三座中の一座（祭神は[[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]（[[事代主命]]））に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 月まの明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
伝承では、[[承和 (日本)|承和]]5年（[[838年]]）頃に[[神津島]]で大噴火が起きたことを受け、この地に遥拝所を建立することになり、ここには[[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]（[[事代主命]]）を祀り、その正后・阿波咩命は[[八幡神社 (下田市吉佐美)|吉佐美（現・八幡神社 合祀 三島神社）]]に、その子・物忌奈命は[[若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)|湊（現・若宮神社）]]にそれぞれ祀られたとされる<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
[[明治]]16年（[[1883年]]）に郷社に列する<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]（[[事代主命]]）
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[八幡神社 (下田市吉佐美)]] * [[若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - 静岡県神社庁

{{nihongo|'''Tsukima Shrine'''|月間神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tsukima Shrine'''|月間神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tsukima Shrine'''|月間神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

revision.comment: 2 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 月間神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[南伊豆町]]手石336 |位置 = {{coord|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]（[[事代主命]]） |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 伝・[[嘉祥]]年間（848年-851年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月2日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=月間神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''月間神社'''（つきまじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[南伊豆町]]手石（ていし）にある[[神社]]<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 南伊豆町の南東、青野川西岸である手石地区の中央に鎮座している。
創建は[[嘉祥]]年間（848年-851年）とされ、[[式内社]]である'''竹麻神社'''（たかま(つくまの)じんじゃ）三座中の一座（祭神は[[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]（[[事代主命]]））に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 月まの明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
伝承では、[[承和 (日本)|承和]]5年（[[838年]]）頃に[[神津島]]で大噴火が起きたことを受け、この地に遥拝所を建立することになり、ここには[[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]（[[事代主命]]）を祀り、その正后・阿波咩命は[[八幡神社 (下田市吉佐美)|吉佐美（現・八幡神社 合祀 三島神社）]]に、その子・物忌奈命は[[若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)|湊（現・若宮神社）]]にそれぞれ祀られたとされる<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
[[明治]]16年（[[1883年]]）に郷社に列する<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]（[[事代主命]]）
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[八幡神社 (下田市吉佐美)]] * [[若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{DEFAULTSORT:つきましんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:南伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:南伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 月間神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[南伊豆町]]手石336 |位置 = {{coord|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]（[[事代主命]]） |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 伝・[[嘉祥]]年間（848年-851年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月2日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=月間神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''月間神社'''（つきまじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[南伊豆町]]手石（ていし）にある[[神社]]<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 南伊豆町の南東、青野川西岸である手石地区の中央に鎮座している。
創建は[[嘉祥]]年間（848年-851年）とされ、[[式内社]]である'''竹麻神社'''（たかま(つくまの)じんじゃ）三座中の一座（祭神は[[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]（[[事代主命]]））に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 月まの明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
伝承では、[[承和 (日本)|承和]]5年（[[838年]]）頃に[[神津島]]で大噴火が起きたことを受け、この地に遥拝所を建立することになり、ここには[[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]（[[事代主命]]）を祀り、その正后・阿波咩命は[[八幡神社 (下田市吉佐美)|吉佐美（現・八幡神社 合祀 三島神社）]]に、その子・物忌奈命は[[若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)|湊（現・若宮神社）]]にそれぞれ祀られたとされる<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
[[明治]]16年（[[1883年]]）に郷社に列する<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]（[[事代主命]]）
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[八幡神社 (下田市吉佐美)]] * [[若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - 静岡県神社庁
[[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:南伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:南伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]
==End Japanese==

伝承では、[[承和 (日本)|承和]]5年（[[838年]]）頃に[[神津島]]で大噴火が起きたことを受け、この地に遥拝所を建立することになり、ここには[[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]（[[事代主命]]）を祀り、その正后・阿波咩命は[[八幡神社 (下田市吉佐美)|吉佐美（現・八幡神社 合祀 三島神社）]]に、その子・物忌奈命は[[若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)|湊（現・若宮神社）]]にそれぞれ祀られたとされる<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
[[明治]]16年（[[1883年]]）に郷社に列する<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[三嶋大社|三嶋大明神]]（[[事代主命]]）
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[八幡神社 (下田市吉佐美)]] * [[若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - 静岡県神社庁
==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:南伊豆町の歴史]] [[Category:南伊豆町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

'''月間神社'''（つきまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|南伊豆町|Q1203741}}手石（ていし）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

== 概要 ==
南伊豆町の南東、青野川西岸である手石地区の中央に鎮座している。

創建は{{jalink|嘉祥|Q1373157}}年間（848年-851年）とされ、{{jalink|式内社}}である'''竹麻神社'''（たかま(つくまの)じんじゃ）三座中の一座（祭神は{{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三嶋大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主命}}））に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 月まの明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

伝承では、{{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}5年（{{jalink|838年|Q23838}}）頃に{{jalink|神津島|Q301669}}で大噴火が起きたことを受け、この地に遥拝所を建立することになり、ここには{{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三嶋大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主命}}）を祀り、その正后・阿波咩命は{{jalink|八幡神社 (下田市吉佐美)|Q134927474|吉佐美（現・八幡神社 合祀 三島神社）}}に、その子・物忌奈命は{{jalink|若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)|Q134930669|湊（現・若宮神社）}}にそれぞれ祀られたとされる<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}16年（{{jalink|1883年|Q7816}}）に郷社に列する<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

== 祭神 ==
* {{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三嶋大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主命}}）

== 脚注 ==
{{Reflist}}

== 関連項目 ==

* {{jalink|若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)|Q134930669}}

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 月間神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|南伊豆町|Q1203741}}手石336
|位置   = {{coord|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三嶋大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主命}}）
|社格   = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
|創建   = 伝・{{jalink|嘉祥|Q1373157}}年間（848年-851年）
|本殿   = 
|別名   =

|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=月間神社|coordinates=
{{coord2|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''月間神社'''（つきまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|南伊豆町|Q1203741}}手石（ていし）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
南伊豆町の南東、青野川西岸である手石地区の中央に鎮座している。

創建は{{jalink|嘉祥|Q1373157}}年間（848年-851年）とされ、{{jalink|式内社}}である'''竹麻神社'''（たかま(つくまの)じんじゃ）三座中の一座（祭神は{{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三嶋大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主命}}））に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 月まの明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

伝承では、{{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}5年（{{jalink|838年|Q23838}}）頃に{{jalink|神津島|Q301669}}で大噴火が起きたことを受け、この地に遥拝所を建立することになり、ここには{{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三嶋大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主命}}）を祀り、その正后・阿波咩命は{{jalink|八幡神社 (下田市吉佐美)|Q134927474|吉佐美（現・八幡神社 合祀 三島神社）}}に、その子・物忌奈命は{{jalink|若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)|Q134930669|湊（現・若宮神社）}}にそれぞれ祀られたとされる<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}16年（{{jalink|1883年|Q7816}}）に郷社に列する<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三嶋大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主命}}）

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 関連項目 ===
* {{jalink|八幡神社 (下田市吉佐美)|Q134927474}}
* {{jalink|若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)|Q134930669}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - 静岡県神社庁

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 月間神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|南伊豆町|Q1203741}}手石336
|位置   = {{coord|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三嶋大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主命}}）
|社格   = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
|創建   = 伝・{{jalink|嘉祥|Q1373157}}年間（848年-851年）
|本殿   = 
|別名   =

|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=月間神社|coordinates=
{{coord2|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''月間神社'''（つきまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|南伊豆町|Q1203741}}手石（ていし）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
南伊豆町の南東、青野川西岸である手石地区の中央に鎮座している。

創建は{{jalink|嘉祥|Q1373157}}年間（848年-851年）とされ、{{jalink|式内社}}である'''竹麻神社'''（たかま(つくまの)じんじゃ）三座中の一座（祭神は{{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三嶋大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主命}}））に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 月まの明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

伝承では、{{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}5年（{{jalink|838年|Q23838}}）頃に{{jalink|神津島|Q301669}}で大噴火が起きたことを受け、この地に遥拝所を建立することになり、ここには{{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三嶋大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主命}}）を祀り、その正后・阿波咩命は{{jalink|八幡神社 (下田市吉佐美)|Q134927474|吉佐美（現・八幡神社 合祀 三島神社）}}に、その子・物忌奈命は{{jalink|若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)|Q134930669|湊（現・若宮神社）}}にそれぞれ祀られたとされる<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}16年（{{jalink|1883年|Q7816}}）に郷社に列する<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三嶋大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主命}}）

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 関連項目 ===

* {{jalink|若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)|Q134930669}}

=== 外部リンク ===

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 月間神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|南伊豆町|Q1203741}}手石336
|位置   = {{coord|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三嶋大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主命}}）
|社格   = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
|創建   = 伝・{{jalink|嘉祥|Q1373157}}年間（848年-851年）
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 11月2日
|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=月間神社|coordinates=
{{coord2|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''月間神社'''（つきまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|南伊豆町|Q1203741}}手石（ていし）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
南伊豆町の南東、青野川西岸である手石地区の中央に鎮座している。

創建は{{jalink|嘉祥|Q1373157}}年間（848年-851年）とされ、{{jalink|式内社}}である'''竹麻神社'''（たかま(つくまの)じんじゃ）三座中の一座（祭神は{{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三嶋大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主命}}））に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 月まの明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

伝承では、{{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}5年（{{jalink|838年|Q23838}}）頃に{{jalink|神津島|Q301669}}で大噴火が起きたことを受け、この地に遥拝所を建立することになり、ここには{{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三嶋大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主命}}）を祀り、その正后・阿波咩命は{{jalink|八幡神社 (下田市吉佐美)|Q134927474|吉佐美（現・八幡神社 合祀 三島神社）}}に、その子・物忌奈命は{{jalink|若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)|Q134930669|湊（現・若宮神社）}}にそれぞれ祀られたとされる<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}16年（{{jalink|1883年|Q7816}}）に郷社に列する<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三嶋大明神}}（{{jalink|事代主命}}）

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 関連項目 ===
* {{jalink|八幡神社 (下田市吉佐美)|Q134927474}}
* {{jalink|若宮神社 (南伊豆町湊)|Q134930669}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - 静岡県神社庁

'''Tsukima Shrine''' (つきまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Teishi, {{jalink|Minamiizu|Q1203741}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Teishi district on the west bank of the Aono River, in southeastern Minamiizu.

Its foundation is said to be in the {{jalink|Kashō|Q1373157}} era (848-851), and it is an old shrine identified with one of the three seats of the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Takama Shrine''' (たかま / つくまの じんじゃ), with deity {{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772|Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}), and is also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087}}'' as “Senior Fourth Rank Upper, Tsukima no Myōjin”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

Tradition says that after a major eruption occurred on {{jalink|Kōzushima|Q301669}} around {{jalink|Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 5 ({{jalink|838|Q23838}}), a distant-worship site was established here. Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) was enshrined here; his principal consort Awanome-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|Kisami (present Hachiman Shrine, merged Mishima Shrine)}}; and his child Monoi-na-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|Minato (present Wakamiya Shrine)}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 16 ({{jalink|1883|Q7816}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772|Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}})

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== Related items ===
* {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474}}
* {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669}}

=== External links ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines

Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) was enshrined here; his principal consort Awanome-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|Kisami (present Hachiman Shrine, merged Mishima Shrine)}}; and his child Monoi-na-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|Minato (present Wakamiya Shrine)}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 16 ({{jalink|1883|Q7816}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===

'''Tsukima Shrine''' (つきまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Teishi, {{jalink|Minamiizu|Q1203741}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Teishi district on the west bank of the Aono River, in southeastern Minamiizu.

Its foundation is said to be in the {{jalink|Kashō|Q1373157}} era (848-851), and it is an old shrine identified with one of the three seats of the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Takama Shrine''' (たかま / つくまの じんじゃ), with deity {{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772|Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}), and is also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087}}'' as “Senior Fourth Rank Upper, Tsukima no Myōjin”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

Tradition says that after a major eruption occurred on {{jalink|Kōzushima|Q301669}} around {{jalink|Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 5 ({{jalink|838|Q23838}}), a distant-worship site was established here. Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) was enshrined here; his principal consort Awanome-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|Kisami (present Hachiman Shrine, merged Mishima Shrine)}}; and his child Monoi-na-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|Minato (present Wakamiya Shrine)}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 16 ({{jalink|1883|Q7816}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772|Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}})

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== Related items ===
* {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474}}
* {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669}}

=== External links ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines

'''Tsukima Shrine''' (つきまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Teishi, {{jalink|Minamiizu|Q1203741}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Teishi district on the west bank of the Aono River, in southeastern Minamiizu.

Its foundation is said to be in the {{jalink|Kashō|Q1373157}} era (848-851), and it is an old shrine identified with one of the three seats of the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Takama Shrine''' (たかま / つくまの じんじゃ), with deity {{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772|Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}), and is also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087}}'' as “Senior Fourth Rank Upper, Tsukima no Myōjin”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

Tradition says that after a major eruption occurred on {{jalink|Kōzushima|Q301669}} around {{jalink|Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 5 ({{jalink|838|Q23838}}), a distant-worship site was established here. Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) was enshrined here; his principal consort Awanome-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|Kisami (present Hachiman Shrine, merged Mishima Shrine)}}; and his child Monoi-na-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|Minato (present Wakamiya Shrine)}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 16 ({{jalink|1883|Q7816}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772|Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}})

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== Related items ===
* {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474}}
* {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669}}

=== External links ===

'''Tsukima Shrine''' (つきまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Teishi, {{jalink|Minamiizu|Q1203741}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Teishi district on the west bank of the Aono River, in southeastern Minamiizu.

Its foundation is said to be in the {{jalink|Kashō|Q1373157}} era (848-851), and it is an old shrine identified with one of the three seats of the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Takama Shrine''' (たかま / つくまの じんじゃ), with deity {{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772|Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}), and is also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087}}'' as “Senior Fourth Rank Upper, Tsukima no Myōjin”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

Tradition says that after a major eruption occurred on {{jalink|Kōzushima|Q301669}} around {{jalink|Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 5 ({{jalink|838|Q23838}}), a distant-worship site was established here. Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) was enshrined here; his principal consort Awanome-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|Kisami (present Hachiman Shrine, merged Mishima Shrine)}}; and his child Monoi-na-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|Minato (present Wakamiya Shrine)}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 16 ({{jalink|1883|Q7816}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E}}}}

'''Tsukima Shrine''' (つきまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Teishi, {{ill|lt=Minamiizu|Q1203741|WD=Q1203741}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Teishi district on the west bank of the Aono River, in southeastern Minamiizu.

Its foundation is said to be in the {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157}} era (848-851), and it is an old shrine identified with one of the three seats of the {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} '''Takama Shrine''' (たかま / つくまの じんじゃ), with deity {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{ill||lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'' as “Senior Fourth Rank Upper, Tsukima no Myōjin”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

Tradition says that after a major eruption occurred on {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|WD=Q301669}} around {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838}}), a distant-worship site was established here. Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) was enshrined here; his principal consort Awanome-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|WD=Q134927474|lt=Kisami (present Hachiman Shrine, merged Mishima Shrine)}}; and his child Monoi-na-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|WD=Q134930669|lt=Minato (present Wakamiya Shrine)}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{ill||lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}})

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== Related items ===
* {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|WD=Q134927474}}
* {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|WD=Q134930669}}

=== External links ===

'''Tsukima Shrine''' (つきまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Teishi, {{ill|lt=Minamiizu|Q1203741|WD=Q1203741}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Teishi district on the west bank of the Aono River, in southeastern Minamiizu.

Its foundation is said to be in the {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157}} era (848-851), and it is an old shrine identified with one of the three seats of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Takama Shrine''' (たかま / つくまの じんじゃ), with deity {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'' as “Senior Fourth Rank Upper, Tsukima no Myōjin”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

Tradition says that after a major eruption occurred on {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|WD=Q301669}} around {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838}}), a distant-worship site was established here. Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) was enshrined here; his principal consort Awanome-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|WD=Q134927474|lt=Kisami (present Hachiman Shrine, merged Mishima Shrine)}}; and his child Monoi-na-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|WD=Q134930669|lt=Minato (present Wakamiya Shrine)}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== Related items ===
* {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|WD=Q134927474}}
* {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|WD=Q134930669}}

'''Tsukima Shrine''' (つきまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Teishi, {{ill|lt=Minamiizu|Q1203741|WD=Q1203741}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Teishi district on the west bank of the Aono River, in southeastern Minamiizu.

Its foundation is said to be in the {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157}} era (848-851), and it is an old shrine identified with one of the three seats of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takama Shrine''' (たかま / つくまの じんじゃ), with deity {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611}}), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'' as “Senior Fourth Rank Upper, Tsukima no Myōjin”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

Tradition says that after a major eruption occurred on {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|WD=Q301669}} around {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838}}), a distant-worship site was established here. Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) was enshrined here; his principal consort Awanome-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|WD=Q134927474|lt=Kisami (present Hachiman Shrine, merged Mishima Shrine)}}; and his child Monoi-na-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|WD=Q134930669|lt=Minato (present Wakamiya Shrine)}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611}})

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== Related items ===
* {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|WD=Q134927474}}
* {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|WD=Q134930669}}

Its foundation is said to be in the {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157}} era (848-851), and it is an old shrine identified with one of the three seats of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takama Shrine''' (たかま / つくまの じんじゃ), with deity {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611}}), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'' as “Senior Fourth Rank Upper, Tsukima no Myōjin”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

Tradition says that after a major eruption occurred on {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|WD=Q301669}} around {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838}}), a distant-worship site was established here. Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) was enshrined here; his principal consort Awanome-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|WD=Q134927474|lt=Kisami (present Hachiman Shrine, merged Mishima Shrine)}}; and his child Monoi-na-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|WD=Q134930669|lt=Minato (present Wakamiya Shrine)}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611}})

'''Tsukima Shrine''' (つきまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Teishi, {{ill|lt=Minamiizu|Q1203741|WD=Q1203741|1=Minamiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Teishi district on the west bank of the Aono River, in southeastern Minamiizu.

Its foundation is said to be in the {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} era (848-851), and it is an old shrine identified with one of the three seats of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takama Shrine''' (たかま / つくまの じんじゃ), with deity {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=Mishima Taisha}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō}}'' as “Senior Fourth Rank Upper, Tsukima no Myōjin”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

Tradition says that after a major eruption occurred on {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|WD=Q301669|1=Kōzu-shima}} around {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838|1=838}}), a distant-worship site was established here. Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) was enshrined here; his principal consort Awanome-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|WD=Q134927474|lt=Kisami (present Hachiman Shrine, merged Mishima Shrine)|1=Hachiman Shrine (Q134927474)}}; and his child Monoi-na-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|WD=Q134930669|lt=Minato (present Wakamiya Shrine)|1=Wakamiya Shrine (Q134930669)}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816|1=1883}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

'''Tsukima Shrine''' (つきまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Teishi, {{ill|lt=Minamiizu|Q1203741|WD=Q1203741|1=Minamiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Teishi district on the west bank of the Aono River, in southeastern Minamiizu.

Its foundation is said to be in the {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} era (848-851), and it is an old shrine identified with one of the three seats of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takama Shrine''' (たかま / つくまの じんじゃ), with deity {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=Mishima Taisha}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō}}'' as “Senior Fourth Rank Upper, Tsukima no Myōjin”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

Tradition says that after a major eruption occurred on {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|WD=Q301669|1=Kōzu-shima}} around {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838|1=838}}), a distant-worship site was established here. Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) was enshrined here; his principal consort Awanome-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|WD=Q134927474|lt=Kisami (present Hachiman Shrine, merged Mishima Shrine)|1=Hachiman Shrine (Q134927474)}}; and his child Monoi-na-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|WD=Q134930669|lt=Minato (present Wakamiya Shrine)|1=Wakamiya Shrine (Q134930669)}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816|1=1883}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=Mishima Taisha}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}})

=== Notes ===

* {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|WD=Q134930669|1=Wakamiya Shrine (Q134930669)}}

'''Tsukima Shrine''' (つきまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Teishi, {{ill|lt=Minamiizu|Q1203741|WD=Q1203741|1=Minamiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Teishi district on the west bank of the Aono River, in southeastern Minamiizu.

Its foundation is said to be in the {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} era (848-851), and it is an old shrine identified with one of the three seats of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takama Shrine''' (たかま / つくまの じんじゃ), with deity {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=Mishima Taisha}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō}}'' as “Senior Fourth Rank Upper, Tsukima no Myōjin”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

Tradition says that after a major eruption occurred on {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|WD=Q301669|1=Kōzu-shima}} around {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838|1=838}}), a distant-worship site was established here. Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) was enshrined here; his principal consort Awanome-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|WD=Q134927474|lt=Kisami (present Hachiman Shrine, merged Mishima Shrine)|1=Hachiman Shrine (Q134927474)}}; and his child Monoi-na-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|WD=Q134930669|lt=Minato (present Wakamiya Shrine)|1=Wakamiya Shrine (Q134930669)}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816|1=1883}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=Mishima Taisha}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}})

=== Notes ===

'''Tsukima Shrine''' (つきまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Teishi, {{ill|lt=Minamiizu|Q1203741|WD=Q1203741|1=Minamiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Teishi district on the west bank of the Aono River, in southeastern Minamiizu.

Its foundation is said to be in the {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} era (848-851), and it is an old shrine identified with one of the three seats of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takama Shrine''' (たかま / つくまの じんじゃ), with deity {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=Mishima Taisha}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō}}'' as “Senior Fourth Rank Upper, Tsukima no Myōjin”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

Tradition says that after a major eruption occurred on {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|WD=Q301669|1=Kōzu-shima}} around {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838|1=838}}), a distant-worship site was established here. Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) was enshrined here; his principal consort Awanome-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|WD=Q134927474|lt=Kisami (present Hachiman Shrine, merged Mishima Shrine)|1=Hachiman Shrine (Q134927474)}}; and his child Monoi-na-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|WD=Q134930669|lt=Minato (present Wakamiya Shrine)|1=Wakamiya Shrine (Q134930669)}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816|1=1883}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=Mishima Taisha}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}})

|社格   = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}

'''Tsukima Shrine''' (つきまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Teishi, {{ill|lt=Minamiizu|Q1203741|WD=Q1203741|1=Minamiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Teishi district on the west bank of the Aono River, in southeastern Minamiizu.

Its foundation is said to be in the {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} era (848-851), and it is an old shrine identified with one of the three seats of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takama Shrine''' (たかま / つくまの じんじゃ), with deity {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=Mishima Taisha}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō}}'' as “Senior Fourth Rank Upper, Tsukima no Myōjin”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

Tradition says that after a major eruption occurred on {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|WD=Q301669|1=Kōzu-shima}} around {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838|1=838}}), a distant-worship site was established here. Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) was enshrined here; his principal consort Awanome-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|WD=Q134927474|lt=Kisami (present Hachiman Shrine, merged Mishima Shrine)|1=Hachiman Shrine (Q134927474)}}; and his child Monoi-na-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|WD=Q134930669|lt=Minato (present Wakamiya Shrine)|1=Wakamiya Shrine (Q134930669)}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816|1=1883}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=Mishima Taisha}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}})

=== Notes ===

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Tsukima Shrine
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Kamo District}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|38|16.5|N|138|52|52.5|E}}}}

|社格   = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}

'''Tsukima Shrine''' (つきまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Teishi, {{ill|lt=Minamiizu|Q1203741|WD=Q1203741|1=Minamiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401084 月間神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined in the center of the Teishi district on the west bank of the Aono River, in southeastern Minamiizu.

Its foundation is said to be in the {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} era (848-851), and it is an old shrine identified with one of the three seats of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takama Shrine''' (たかま / つくまの じんじゃ), with deity {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=Mishima Taisha}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō}}'' as “Senior Fourth Rank Upper, Tsukima no Myōjin”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/tukima_title.htm 月間神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140142-01.html 月間神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

Tradition says that after a major eruption occurred on {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|WD=Q301669|1=Kōzu-shima}} around {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838|1=838}}), a distant-worship site was established here. Mishima Daimyōjin (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) was enshrined here; his principal consort Awanome-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Kisami, Shimoda)|Q134927474|WD=Q134927474|lt=Kisami (present Hachiman Shrine, merged Mishima Shrine)|1=Hachiman Shrine (Q134927474)}}; and his child Monoi-na-no-Mikoto was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine (Minamiizu Minato)|Q134930669|WD=Q134930669|lt=Minato (present Wakamiya Shrine)|1=Wakamiya Shrine (Q134930669)}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816|1=1883}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=Mishima Taisha}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}})
